acceptance_targets: [chapter_17_craft_001] Between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:479 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:493, the revised text must include at least one fully dramatized beat of sex after they reach the bed: concrete physical action, bodily response from both characters, and a visible expression of the new terms of trust/work. Fail if the passage still moves from undressing/kissing directly to 'Later' or only implies the act through bed, lamp, or blanket imagery.

[FBR-011] Pass if both cited lines no longer use editorial hinge phrasing and the temptation remains unmistakable through the documents and Silas's material craving; fail if the chapter still names the interpretive turn for the reader.

findings.acceptance_test: Between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:479 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:493, the revised text must include at least one fully dramatized beat of sex after they reach the bed: concrete physical action, bodily response from both characters, and a visible expression of the new terms of trust/work. Fail if the passage still moves from undressing/kissing directly to 'Later' or only implies the act through bed, lamp, or blanket imagery.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:479; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:491; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:493 — the scene gets them to the bed, gives a few setup gestures, then jumps straight to 'Later' once sex is imminent.
findings.problem: The chapter commits to practical, erotic, and ethical renegotiation, then sanitizes the consummation at the exact point presence is required. After spending the whole scene earning bodily specificity and mutual terms, the narrative retreats into a classic intimacy fade-to-black, which undercuts the constitution's anti-sanitization standard and weakens the chapter's largest private consequence.
findings.rewrite_direction: Expand the bedroom passage beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:479 and carrying through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:493 so the sex itself is rendered on-page rather than implied. Keep the existing room details (lamp, drying Ferrymouth board, ledgers, thin walls), but make them shape the encounter: show how Inez's rules govern touch, pace, speech, and whether Silas stays present instead of cutting from foreplay to aftermath.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if both cited lines no longer use editorial hinge phrasing and the temptation remains unmistakable through the documents and Silas's material craving; fail if the chapter still names the interpretive turn for the reader.
findings.problem: Full-book explanatory hinge pattern ('There it was' / 'That landed') appears in 13 chapters. At 6408 and 6486 the narration again steps outside the scene to identify the bait in Gideon's offer instead of letting the desk papers and Silas's hunger make the temptation visible.
findings.rewrite_direction: REPLACE the hinge sentences at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6408 and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:6486. Trust the mock-ups, the salary figures, Junie's notes, and Gideon's diction to show the seduction and insult. Keep Silas's conflict in the body and in the paper, not in explanatory bridge lines.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the book's strange civic biology, vulgar humor, erotic consequence, and class abrasion. When a choice appears between the cleaner version and the truer, messier one, choose the messier version so long as causality and character truth remain exact. Treat the city's slime, smell, heat, and appetite as part of the novel's beauty rather than embarrassment to be polished away.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If sex, shame, hunger, class humiliation, bodily mess, or economic pressure starts drifting into decorous vagueness, rewrite until the scene carries concrete sensory and social cost on the page.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm across the novel; uncontracted forms should signal emphasis, class performance, ceremonial language, or Gideon's deliberate control.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate crowd scenes with cleanup, ritual with labor, public humiliation with private tenderness, and single-sweep chapters with multi-scene accumulations; no two consecutive chapters should feel built from the same emotional weather, dialogue density, or scene rhythm.
prose_style_profile.diction: Period-aware but not museum-stiff; vivid, bodily, class-conscious, and willing to be ugly or funny without slipping into arch parody or modern snark.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person anchored to Silas Vale for the full novel, with no head-hopping and no neutral historian voice; Inez remains legible through observed action, dialogue, and the objects she shares.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Elastic and pressure-sensitive: quick comic snap in public scenes, thicker sensory accumulation in labor and erotic passages, and hard clean sentences at moments of shame or decision.
